exemplar.title: To what extent will Appleâs 2030 agenda contribute to improving its global ecological impact?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent will Appleâs 2030 agenda contribute to improving its global ecological impact?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicitly identifies and consistently revisits âSustainabilityâ as the key concept throughout the IA.
Strong connection to Apple, analysing how EU regulation and circular models drive the firmâs ecological agenda.
Clear analytical thinking in STEEPLE, circularâmodel discussion and CSR data, demonstrating robust conceptual understanding.
weaknesses: Occasional generic statements dilute seamless integration of the concept in every analytical paragraph.
Definition of sustainability remains generic; deeper framework integration (e.g., Triple Bottom Line) is lacking.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments locate the work at band 4 for demonstrating strong understanding and partial integration of sustainability. The concept frames most analysis, but some descriptive passages and generic language prevent full integration required for band 5. A score of 4/5 accurately reflects this strong yet not fully embedded performance.

strengths: Includes four supporting documents meeting the 3â5 requirement for depth and range.
Documents are relevantâApple CSR reports, IPE critique and industry articleâand integrated into the analysis.
Citations provide useful figures and contrasting viewpoints to support arguments.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on Appleâs own reports limits independent perspective.
Depth of external sources is adequate but lacks broader academic or industry diversity required for top band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA meets band 3 by supplying four relevant documents and linking them to the analysis. However, the limited source variety and depth keep it from the broader in-depth range of band 4. A score of 3/4 aligns with the strand reasoning of ârelevant and sufficiently in-depth.â

strengths: Methodology clearly outlines STEEPLE and circular business models as analytical tools.
Tools are generally applied to the research questionâlegal factors analysed, recycling linked to circularity.
Selection aligns with syllabus expectations, providing an adequate toolkit for analysis.
weaknesses: Rationale for choosing STEEPLE and circular models is brief and underdeveloped.
Focus on legal factors only is narrow; a fuller STEEPLE scope or justification would strengthen relevance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand evidence indicates an adequate (band 3) selection and application of tools. While tools are applied effectively to key issues, the rationale and scope are limited, preventing the âeffectiveâ band 4. A score of 3/4 reflects this moderate but acceptable use of theories.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of quantitative data (e.g., 2.6 million ton emission reduction, 40 000 ton e-waste reduction) to support analysis.
Logical links between regulation, circular initiatives and ecological impact show mostly effective analysis.
Integration of 2024 data updates strengthens temporal dimension of the evaluation.
weaknesses: Lacks deeper critical evaluation of feasibility and potential challenges to Appleâs 2030 goals.
Limited discussion of methodological credibility and cost-benefit trade-offs reduces analytical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates sufficient data selection and mostly effective analysis consistent with band 4. It integrates quantitative and qualitative evidence to evaluate Appleâs ecological impact, though it falls short of the sustained depth and triangulation of band 5. Thus, 4/5 accurately reflects its strong but improvable analysis.

strengths: Conclusions synthesize evidence from STEEPLE, circular models and emissions data in a coherent way.
Final paragraph explicitly answers the research question with a qualified judgment on Appleâs ecological prospects.
No inconsistencies appear between conclusions and the presented evidence.
weaknesses: Lacks actionable recommendations or implications for Appleâs future strategy.
Could more explicitly restate the research question to reinforce focus and coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place conclusions at band 3: fully consistent with evidence and directly answering the research question. Although the conclusion could be deepened with recommendations, it meets the top band descriptor. A score of 3/3 is therefore warranted.

strengths: Logical organization with contents page, clear headings and sub-sections mirroring the methodology.
Appropriate structure throughout, guiding the reader effectively from introduction to conclusion.
Research question is clearly formulated and frames the investigation.
weaknesses: Redundant summary paragraph before the conclusion disrupts narrative flow.
No introductory roadmap outlining the methodology and analysis sections to preface structure.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate and consistent structure meeting band 2 descriptors. Despite minor redundancies, it maintains clarity and organization throughout. Awarding 2/2 aligns with the evidence of strong structural coherence.

strengths: All required elements are present: title page, word count, table of contents, supporting documents and bibliography.
Formatting of key elements (e.g., word count on title page) adheres to IB presentation guidelines.
weaknesses: Inconsistent footnote formatting and citation styles across bibliography entries.
Appendix visuals embedded in main text rather than placed and referenced properly in appendices.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the highest band as all required elements are included, fulfilling IB formal requirements. Formatting inconsistencies and appendix placement issues are minor, so a full 2/2 reflects the largely professional presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure with a contents page, headings and sub-sections that mirror the methodology.  Flow is clear, meeting the âappropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor (top band 2 marks).

The documents are relevant and referenced inside the analysis, providing useful figures and contrasting views.  Depth is adequate but not extensive; reliance on Appleâs own CSR reports limits range.  Hence ârelevant and sufficiently in-depth,â not the broad range needed for band 4.
Title page, table of contents, word count, supporting documents and bibliography are all present.  Required elements appear complete, so the highest presentation band is justified.
Four supporting documents are listed and cited (Apple 2023 report, IPE critique, Apple 2024 report, Data-centre article), meeting the 3-5 range required for this band.

Tools are generally applied to the research question: legal factors analysed via STEEPLE; circular model linked to recycling programmes; statistics tied to emissions progress.  Relevance is clear in most places, though some sections drift into description, so effectiveness is moderate rather than fully effectiveâmatching band 3.

Multiple quantitative data sets (emission tonnage, waste tonnage, energy use) and qualitative information (EU regulation) are selected and used to support points.  The selection is broad enough and generally integrated, warranting âsufficientâ rather than merely âadequateâ use.

Analysis explains how legal pressure, recycling initiatives and emissions trends affect Appleâs ecological impact, and briefly evaluates credibility (IPE critique).  Logical links are mostly present, though depth and triangulation could be stronger, so âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ (band 4) is appropriate.

The final paragraph explicitly answers the research questionâApple likely to improve ecological impact but impact may be overstatedâmaking a clear, qualified judgement.  This matches the descriptor for a full answer (band 3).

The student selects STEEPLE, circular business models and descriptive statisticsâan adequate, syllabus-aligned toolkit, though lacking more advanced strategic or financial models.  This fits the âadequate selectionâ descriptor.

The conclusion synthesises evidence from STEEPLE, circular models and emissions data and remains consistent with what was analysed (e.g., mixed results, data credibility).  No contradictions appear, satisfying the top band for this criterion (3 marks).

Apple is consistently foregrounded: the research question, STEEPLE legal focus, circularâmodel initiatives, emissions statistics and opposing viewpoints all relate directly to the firm.  Connections are analysed (e.g. EU battery law forcing change, supplier data gaps).  However, occasional generic statements appear (âtechnology firms faceâ¦â) and integration is not seamless across the whole IA, so the descriptor for partial rather than full integration (band 4) is most accurate.

The student clearly identifies sustainability as the key concept, provides a working definition, and revisits it when discussing STEEPLE, circular models and CSR data.  Throughout the commentary the concept frames the discussion, yet it is not explicitly woven into every analytical paragraph (e.g. some data discussion slips into a purely descriptive tone).  This aligns with âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysis,â not the fully embedded integration required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The methodology section clearly outlines STEEPLE and circular models as tools, establishing a foundation for structured analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly and concisely formulated, framing the investigation effectively and guiding the focus of the analysis.
The student explicitly identifies âSustainabilityâ as the key concept, establishing a clear conceptual focus that is revisited throughout the analysis.
The inclusion of word count on the title page fulfills IB requirements and demonstrates adherence to formal submission guidelines.
The contents page is comprehensive and logically organized, helping the reader navigate the IAâs structure effectively.
The introduction would benefit from tighter linkage to the research question: explicitly state how the background information leads into the focus on Appleâs 2030 ecological agenda.
Consider adding a brief roadmap to the introduction outlining methodology and analysis sections to enhance overall structural clarity.
The definition of sustainability is accurate but generic; incorporate an established sustainability framework (e.g., Brundtland, Triple Bottom Line) to deepen conceptual integration.
Listing the 2030 goals provides clarity but lacks critical evaluation; consider analysing the feasibility and potential challenges of each target.
Footnotes display HTML tags (<sup>) inconsistently; standardize formatting to meet academic citation conventions in the main text.
Rationale for selecting STEEPLE and circular business models is brief; expand on why these tools effectively address the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The syllabus links appear densely formatted; separate each IB syllabus reference and briefly explain its relevance for clarity.
The STEEPLE analysis focuses solely on legal factors; either justify this narrow scope or expand to address political, economic, social, technological, environmental, and ethical aspects.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Quantify the environmental benefit of using 85% recycled cobalt (e.g., estimated COâ reduction) to strengthen the analytical linkage between regulation and impact.
Internal operations strategies lack discussion of cost implications; incorporate analysis of trade-offs between environmental benefits and financial investment.
All supporting evidence is drawn from Apple reports; consider integrating independent sources or academic studies to deepen document relevance.
The explanation of a 2.6 million ton emission reduction demonstrates strong use of quantitative data to support analysis.
The description of 40 000 tons of e-waste reduction provides compelling evidence of circular model effectiveness.
Appendix visuals appear embedded in the main text; relocate full figures to appendices and reference them within the analysis for better clarity.
The IPE critique is referenced but lacks appraisal of its research methodology; critically evaluate its credibility and potential bias.
Updating emissions data with the 2024 report effectively strengthens the paperâs temporal analysis of progress.
Ensure the figure includes clear axis labels and a legend to guide interpretation when moved to appendices.
The summary paragraph before the conclusion repeats earlier analysis; streamline this section to avoid redundancy and maintain narrative flow.
The conclusion is clearly labeled and distinct, providing a dedicated section to synthesize findings.
The conclusion summarizes findings but could be enhanced with actionable recommendations or implications for Appleâs future strategies.
The final discussion could more explicitly restate and answer the research question to reinforce the paperâs focus and coherence.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent formatting (URLs, styles); adopt a single citation style throughout to enhance professionalism.
